_provenance.curated_subset: 11 styles — curated, not the full 67-style catalogue
_provenance.note: Adapted named aesthetics from ui-ux-pro-max styles.csv, each with a when_not_to_use cue. Only static-graphic-relevant styles kept.

items.name: Swiss / International
items.description: Grid-driven, objective, lots of breathing room.
items.when_not_to_use: playful or artistic brands that need warmth and personality

items.name: Bold Typographic
items.when_not_to_use: dense, data-heavy panels where the words must share space with figures

items.name: Minimal Mono
items.description: Restrained, calm, one accent does the work.
items.when_not_to_use: brands that need energy, warmth, or playfulness

items.name: Geometric Flat
items.when_not_to_use: luxury or heritage brands that need richness and depth

items.name: Data-Forward
items.description: One number is the hero of each panel.
items.when_not_to_use: narrative or emotional storytelling with little data

items.name: Brutalism
items.description: Raw, high-contrast, deliberately unpolished and attention-grabbing.
items.when_not_to_use: trust-first finance, healthcare, or accessibility-led contexts

items.name: Glassmorphism
items.when_not_to_use: low-contrast or text-heavy graphics where legibility must win

items.name: Vibrant & Block-based
items.description: Bold colour blocks and confident contrast.

items.name: Dark Mode (OLED)
items.description: True-dark surfaces with a single luminous accent.
items.when_not_to_use: light, airy lifestyle brands or print-bound graphics

items.name: Accessible & Ethical
items.description: Clarity and contrast first, decoration second.
items.when_not_to_use: only swap when a brief explicitly demands an expressive, lower-contrast look
